vitals.date_specific: Defunct. Final edition ran April 6-9, 2011; the planned 47th edition (2012) was suspended by organizer Germatur Gestion citing insufficient financing, and it never returned.
vitals.start_format: Multi-day UCI-ranked stage race for Elite/U23 men and UCI Continental teams — not an open-entry mass-participation event

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 56. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 54.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — this profile describes a defunct elite UCI stage race, not a gran fondo, and it is NOT the same event as Mallorca 312 (an active, open-entry modern cyclosportive that this profile's data was previously conflated with). The Cinturon Ciclista a Mallorca ran 1964-2011 as a multi-day stage race around the island, restricted to Elite/U23 riders and UCI Continental trade teams -- entered the UCI Europe Tour in 2005 and was classified UCI 2.2 in its final years. The final (46th) edition, April 6-9 2011, comprised a 7.1km individual time trial followed by road stages of 148.5km, 147.9km, and 144km (447.5km total). It folded when the organizer suspended the planned 2012 edition over insufficient financing and never returned.
course_description.signature_challenge: A four-stage format (individual time trial + three ~145-150km road stages) rather than any single defining climb or course feature.

logistics.airport: Historically Palma de Mallorca (PMI), though the event no longer runs.

history.origin_story: First held in 1964, won by Francisco Juan Granell. Ran as a multi-day amateur/elite stage race around Mallorca for nearly five decades, entering the UCI Europe Tour in 2005 (classified UCI 2.2 in its final editions, restricted to Elite/U23 and UCI Continental teams).
notable_moments: Notable winners across its history: Sean Kelly (1976), Vladimir Karpets (2000), Bradley Wiggins (2001), Sergio Henao (2009)
Frederik Bertelsen was the only two-time winner, in 1994 and 1995
Final (46th) edition ran April 6-9, 2011 -- Iker Camano won overall ahead of Tadej Valjavec and Ian Bibby
The planned 2012 (47th) edition was suspended by organizer Germatur Gestion, citing insufficient financing, and never returned
history.reputation: A genuinely historic race with an internationally credible winners' list (Kelly, Wiggins, Karpets, Henao) that ran for nearly 50 years before the organizer suspended it in 2012, citing insufficient financing.

biased_opinion.verdict: Historic and Genuinely Defunct — Not a Gran Fondo, Not Mallorca 312
biased_opinion.summary: The Cinturon Ciclista a Mallorca was a real, nearly 50-year-old UCI-ranked elite/U23 stage race with a legitimate winners' list including Sean Kelly, Bradley Wiggins, and Sergio Henao -- and it has been defunct since 2011. This profile previously conflated it with Mallorca 312, an entirely different, currently-active, open-entry mass-participation cyclosportive that began separately in 2010 and is not restricted to elite/U23 riders or trade teams. The two events briefly coexisted (2010-11) under different organizers and were never the same race. There is nothing to book here -- this is a historical record, not a live event.
strengths: Genuinely historic pedigree -- a UCI Europe Tour race with a winners' list including Sean Kelly, Bradley Wiggins, Vladimir Karpets, and Sergio Henao
Ran for nearly five decades (1964-2011) before folding, a long and legitimate competitive history
weaknesses: Defunct since 2011 -- there is no current edition to race, and none has returned in 15 years

Was never a gran fondo or open-entry sportive -- an elite/U23 UCI-ranked stage race for licensed riders and Continental teams only, which the previous 'gran_fondo' framing on this profile misrepresented
This profile's data was previously conflated with Mallorca 312, a completely different, still-active modern event -- do not confuse the two
biased_opinion.bottom_line: This is a historical entry, not a live race. If you're looking for a current Mallorca cycling challenge, this database's mallorca-312 profile covers the actual active event -- do not conflate the two.

final_verdict.one_liner: A historic 1964-2011 elite UCI stage race, long defunct -- not a gran fondo, not Mallorca 312.
final_verdict.should_you_race: You can't -- this event ended in 2011 and never returned. If you want a current Mallorca cycling challenge, look at Mallorca 312, a genuinely distinct, active, open-entry event.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a current, open-entry Mallorca cyclosportive, see Mallorca 312 (this database's separate mallorca-312 profile) -- it began in 2010 as an unrelated event and remains active.

eligibility.notes: Final (46th) edition ran April 6-9, 2011. The planned 2012 (47th) edition was suspended, not formally cancelled outright, by organizer Germatur Gestion citing insufficient financing; it never returned in 15 years. Confirmed genuinely distinct from Mallorca 312 (active, unrelated modern cyclosportive, began separately in 2010) -- do not conflate. Purged this wave: the profile's entire youtube_data block (videos/quotes/rider_intel) previously described Mallorca 312 and the Sa Calobra climb, not this event -- a severe identity conflation, now removed.

catalog_flags.taxonomy_note: Not a gran fondo -- historically a UCI 2.2-classified elite/U23 men's stage race for Continental trade teams (1964-2011), not an open-entry mass-participation event. fondo_rating.discipline left as gran_fondo per rule (never edit the discipline enum directly); flagging here for a human call on whether this defunct pro stage race belongs in the gran_fondo-scored catalog at all.
